content.text:
Stage all changes and create a git commit.

1. Run `git status` to review what's changed
2. Run `git add -A` to stage everything
3. If  is provided, use it as the commit message
4. Otherwise, write a concise conventional commit message based on the diff (feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor)
5. Run `git commit -m "<message>"`

attachment.content:
- commit: Stage all changes and create a git commit
- push: Push current branch to remote
- ship: Stage, commit, and push in one step
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Good, I have a clear picture. Here's my plan:

- `/voronoi` page with a canvas where clicking places a "site" point
- Each site has its own distance metric: L1 (Manhattan), L2 (Euclidean), L3, L½, L∞ (Chebyshev)
- Voronoi cells computed by brute-force pixel scan: for each pixel, find the closest site *using that site's own metric*
- Site labels drawn on top, right-click to remove a site
- Toolbar to select which metric gets applied to the *next* placed point

The interesting part is the boundary shapes: Euclidean → circular fronts, Manhattan → diamond fronts, Chebyshev → square fronts — and where two different metrics meet, the boundaries are asymmetric curves.

content.text:
The page is live. The build is clean and the route is serving correctly.

Here's what was built:

**`/voronoi`** — click anywhere to drop a site point, right-click to remove one.

- **Toolbar** shows the 5 metric buttons (L2, L1, L∞, L3, L½) — the selected one applies to the *next* point you place.
- **Rendering**: brute-force per-pixel in CSS-resolution space, scaled up to the physical canvas. For each pixel, the winner is whichever site has the smallest distance *under that site's own metric*.
- **Cell borders** are darkened with a second neighbor-check pass, so the boundaries between cells are clearly visible.
- **Labels** on each site marker show which metric it uses.

The interesting visual effect: where an L2 site meets an L1 site, the boundary is a curved line tilted toward the Manhattan diamond's flat edges, not a straight perpendicular bisector.

message.content: Nice, great job. Now I want to be able to edit all the points put on a canvas and move them and be able to change the metric.

content.text:
The key interactions to add:
- **Click on a site** → select it (ring indicator), toolbar shows *its* metric
- **Drag a site** → moves it, redraws in real time
- **Click a metric button while site selected** → changes that site's metric
- **Click empty canvas** → deselects (and places a new point on mouseup)
- **Right-click a site** → remove it

For drag performance I'll render at half-resolution during the drag and snap to full-res on mouseup.

content.text:
Clean build. Here's what changed:

**Selecting:** clicking on or near an existing site selects it (white ring indicator). The toolbar metric buttons switch to showing that site's current metric.

**Moving:** drag a selected (or any) site — it moves in real time. During the drag, rendering runs at half resolution for smooth performance; on mouse-release it snaps to full resolution.

**Changing metric:** with a site selected, clicking any metric button changes that site's metric (and re-renders). Without a selection, it sets the metric for the next point you place.

**Cursor feedback:** hovers over a site show `grab`, active drag shows `grabbing`, empty canvas shows `crosshair`.

**Right-click** still removes the nearest site.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: Why it's pixelated? Make it nicer.

content.text: The issue is that Voronoi is computed at CSS resolution and then scaled up to the physical canvas with `imageSmoothingEnabled = false` — so every computed pixel becomes a DPR×DPR block on a retina display. The fix: switch to bilinear upscaling, which also naturally anti-aliases the cell borders.

lastPrompt: Why it's pixelated? Make it nicer.

aiTitle: Interactive Voronoi diagram with custom distance metrics

content.text: The core bug is on line 139: `imageSmoothingEnabled = false` — so every computed CSS pixel becomes a hard DPR×DPR block on retina screens. Switching to bilinear upscaling also naturally anti-aliases the dark border pixels.

content.text: Clean. The bilinear filter does two things at once: scales the image to the physical DPR resolution without blocky pixels, and softly blends the dark border pixels into their neighbors — turning jagged 1px staircases into smooth anti-aliased cell boundaries. During drag it uses `'low'` (faster bilinear), and at rest it uses `'high'` (bicubic).

content.text:
Stage all changes and create a git commit.

1. Run `git status` to review what's changed
2. Run `git add -A` to stage everything
3. If  is provided, use it as the commit message
4. Otherwise, write a concise conventional commit message based on the diff (feat/fix/chore/docs/refactor)
5. Run `git commit -m "<message>"`

lastPrompt: Why it's pixelated? Make it nicer.

aiTitle: Interactive Voronoi diagram with custom distance metrics

input.command:
git add app/voronoi/voronoi-canvas.tsx && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add site editing (drag, metric change) and fix pixelation

- Click a site to select it; drag to move; click a metric button to reassign
- Half-res fast render during drag, full-res on release
- Fix pixelated rendering by switching to bilinear upscaling (imageSmoothingEnabled=true)
  which also anti-aliases cell borders naturally

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Stage and commit voronoi canvas changes
